1. No poverty.summary: No supporting evidence was found to determine the proportion of the population living below the international poverty line. The retrieved context primarily focuses on the Euroclima+ Project 097, land tenure, and sustainability monitoring, but does not provide relevant information on poverty levels. The context mentions various statistics such as the number of project participants (239), the size of each planting wave, the number of farmers interviewed (47), but these numbers do not measure the indicator variable directly. Therefore, the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

CL dated 02/19: T Client  Response:  As  farmers  are  allowed  to  participate  each  year  to  plantation  activities,  the  number  per  year  could  be  different  from  the  total  number of  participants  in the  overall  project  –  239. For example, between plantation year 2012 and plantation year 2013 a same producer could  be  participating  but  he  would  not  be  counted  twice  in  the  final  overall  number  of  unique  participants (239).   his  difference  allows  to  both  have  a  vision  of  the  size  of  each  planting  wave  according  to  the  number  of  participants  per  year  and  of  the  size  of  the  project  according  to  the  number  of  participants in the overall period of the project.

1. No poverty.indicator: 1.1.1 Proportion of the population living below the international poverty line by sex, age, employment status and geographic location (urban/rural)

1. No poverty.summary: No supporting evidence was found in the provided context to determine the proportion of the population living below the national poverty line, by sex and age. The context primarily discusses the project's progress on land tenure, leakage surveys, and updates to the root-to-shoot ratio and CF value, but does not provide any information related to poverty levels or demographics. The only relevant information is a mention of 47 farmers being interviewed, but no data is provided on their socioeconomic status or poverty levels. Therefore, the evidence fails to satisfy the measurement requirement of the indicator.

updated document do not match the information provided in the baseline and project carbon stocks  calculations documents.: -   o   F   I Client  Response:    The  survey  is  conducted  after  a  minimum  of  3  years  from  the  plantation  completion  which  means  that  more  than  3  years  had  passed  between  plantation  and  leakage  monitoring,  thus  ensuring  that  even  the  leakage  events  that  had  not  happened  right  after  plantations would be taken into account.   -  As a matter of fact, leakage monitoring surveys were mostly conducted in 2018 and 2019 in  the framework of biomass inventories which is consistent with this “more than 3 years” rule.  -  The selection of parcels on which leakage surveys were conducted were selected under the  following criteria:  o  Planting models (pure stand or agroforestry)  o  Previous use of soils (cocoa farming / pasture)  o  Diversity of plantation waves, with parcels from each stratum (the sampling was based on  the selection of parcels used for biomass inventories)  In total 47 farmers were interviewed.    The  main  conclusions  of  leakage  surveys  (around  20%  of  producers  interviewed  -  40%  of  them with Model 3 - 45 % with Model 2 and 15% with Model 4) are the following:    For Model 3, displacement of pre-project activities is not a concern and surveys confirm that  this  practice  is  nil  outside  farmer's  lands  propriety.  In  fact,  a  majority  of  farmers  used  to  adopt  a  rotative fallow/annual crops and availability of land is not an issue in the project area.   o  For Model 2 (Cocoa - perennial crops), as the crops was framed in the context of Aprosacao  cocoa  association  and  market  creation  opportunities  (Halba),  no  displacement  of  pre-project  activities was monitored, as farmers find this new activity more appealing (because more profitable  and more structured by the support of technical team from Aprosacao). They are not displacing but  replacing pre-project activities.  o   For  Model  4,  like  for  Model  3,  interviewed  farmers  have  a  different  land  for  pasture  available, no displacement of pre-project activities was observed.  ollowing this finding and internal discussion on appropriate timing to monitor leakage, the project  proponent  has taken  the decision to systematically integrate  leakage monitoring in the  plantation  follow up procedure in the future (monitoring 2, conducted on each parcel 6 months to 1 year after  plantation). Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth. updated document do not match the information provided in the baseline and project carbon stocks  calculations documents.: -   o   F   I Client  Response:    The  survey  is  conducted  after  a  minimum  of  3  years  from  the  plantation  completion  which  means  that  more  than  3  years  had  passed  between  plantation  and  leakage  monitoring,  thus  ensuring  that  even  the  leakage  events  that  had  not  happened  right  after  plantations would be taken into account.   -  As a matter of fact, leakage monitoring surveys were mostly conducted in 2018 and 2019 in  the framework of biomass inventories which is consistent with this “more than 3 years” rule.  -  The selection of parcels on which leakage surveys were conducted were selected under the  following criteria:  o  Planting models (pure stand or agroforestry)  o  Previous use of soils (cocoa farming / pasture)  o  Diversity of plantation waves, with parcels from each stratum (the sampling was based on  the selection of parcels used for biomass inventories)  In total 47 farmers were interviewed.    The  main  conclusions  of  leakage  surveys  (around  20%  of  producers  interviewed  -  40%  of  them with Model 3 - 45 % with Model 2 and 15% with Model 4) are the following:    For Model 3, displacement of pre-project activities is not a concern and surveys confirm that  this  practice  is  nil  outside  farmer's  lands  propriety.  In  fact,  a  majority  of  farmers  used  to  adopt  a  rotative fallow/annual crops and availability of land is not an issue in the project area.   o  For Model 2 (Cocoa - perennial crops), as the crops was framed in the context of Aprosacao  cocoa  association  and  market  creation  opportunities  (Halba),  no  displacement  of  pre-project  activities was monitored, as farmers find this new activity more appealing (because more profitable  and more structured by the support of technical team from Aprosacao). They are not displacing but  replacing pre-project activities.  o   For  Model  4,  like  for  Model  3,  interviewed  farmers  have  a  different  land  for  pasture  available, no displacement of pre-project activities was observed.  ollowing this finding and internal discussion on appropriate timing to monitor leakage, the project  proponent  has taken  the decision to systematically integrate  leakage monitoring in the  plantation  follow up procedure in the future (monitoring 2, conducted on each parcel 6 months to 1 year after  plantation). Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

2. Zero hunger.summary: No supporting evidence was found in the provided context to determine the change in farmer income as a result of the project, by gender and indigenous status. The context mentions the distribution of 49,998 trees to farmers, monitoring of tree survival, and technical recommendations for tree maintenance, but it does not provide any quantitative data on the income of farmers. Additionally, the context mentions training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of women and youth, but it does not provide any quantitative data on the impact of the project on the income of farmers. The context also mentions that 47 farmers were interviewed, but it does not provide any data on the income of these farmers or how it changed as a result of the project. Therefore, the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

:  | : The Aprosacao Reforestation Project is a community project implemented in small scale farms located  in  the  region  of  Olancho,  Honduras.  The  project  aims  to  bring  multiple  social  and  environmental  benefits  by  restoring  forest  cover  in  pastures  and  degraded  areas,  under  four  different  models  of  plantation  (agroforestry  combined  with  cocoa  on  linear  or  intercropping plantations;  pure stand  and  sylvopasture).  The  project  is  developed  by  Pur  Development,  the  project  developer  and  owner,  in  association with Aprosacao Association, the core local implementer organization.   Most of the farmers participating in the project are members of Aprosacao, but the project is open to  the  participation  of  other  local  farmers.  Given  the  increasing  number  of members of  the  association  year  by  year,  the  Aprosacao  project  has  been  designed  to  include  additional  modelling  units  in  the  validated  project  area  until  2020,  each  modelling  unit  defined  following  the  year  of  plantation  and  planting model.   ECOCERT  SA  has  conducted  the  first  verification  audit  before  transition  of  the  project  from  the  previous version to the Gold Standard for Global Goals following a request from the project developer  Pur  Development.    The  purpose  of  the  audit  was  to assess  the  extent  to  which  actual  carbon  stocks  reported in the updated project documentation of the Aprosacao project  are materially accurate and  the  extent  to  which  methods  and  procedures,  including  sustainability  monitoring  procedures,  have  been implemented in accordance with the registered project documentation.    To conduct the audit ECOCERT realized a pre-verification assessment consistent in a remote desk audit  of  the  project  documentation  and  supporting  information,  a  field  visit  and  stakeholder  interviews  process and a documentary review. The Project Documentation was assessed against following criteria:  Gold  Standard  for  ISO  14064-2:2006,  Afforestation/Reforestation  (A/R)  Gold  Standard  Requirements  Version 0.9 August 2013, Applicable GS LUF Program Updates, A/R CDM Combined tool to identify the  baseline  scenario  and  demonstrate  additionality  in  A/R  CDM  project  activities  v1.0,  Other  Applicable  A/R CDM tools according to GS requirements, Other applicable guidance according to GS requirements  (e.g. IPCC Guidelines for National GHG Inventories, ILO Conventions).   Following the results of validation  activities described  above 8  CARs,  1  CL  and 3 FAR  were  identified.  The revised final documents submitted to ECOCERT for review resulted in the closure of all open CARs.  The audit team has no restrictions or uncertainties with respect to the compliance of the project with  all the GS A/R standard criteria and requirements. The project is being appropriately implemented and  monitored. CO2 certificates benefit was calculated correctly on the basis of the information contained  in the registered GS5012 project documentation.     In opinion of ECOCERT the actual carbon stocks of the Aprosacao Project for the period 15-June-2012  to  15-December-2018  are  fairly  stated  in  the  updated  CO2-fixation  document  and  ex-post  removals  calculations documents v1.4 dated 30 September 2019.  ECOCERT verified that the CO2 certificates in  the  relevant  reporting  period  are  equivalent  to  14  924  tCO2e  after  buffer  and  confirms  that  this  is  below the maximum project’s expected carbon stocks equivalent to 51 876 validated CO2 certificates  after buffer defined in the GS5012 registered project documentation. | :

CL dated 02/19: T Client  Response:  As  farmers  are  allowed  to  participate  each  year  to  plantation  activities,  the  number  per  year  could  be  different  from  the  total  number of  participants  in the  overall  project  –  239. For example, between plantation year 2012 and plantation year 2013 a same producer could  be  participating  but  he  would  not  be  counted  twice  in  the  final  overall  number  of  unique  participants (239).   his  difference  allows  to  both  have  a  vision  of  the  size  of  each  planting  wave  according  to  the  number  of  participants  per  year  and  of  the  size  of  the  project  according  to  the  number  of  participants in the overall period of the project.

:  | : The Aprosacao Reforestation Project is a community project implemented in small scale farms located  in  the  region  of  Olancho,  Honduras.  The  project  aims  to  bring  multiple  social  and  environmental  benefits  by  restoring  forest  cover  in  pastures  and  degraded  areas,  under  four  different  models  of  plantation  (agroforestry  combined  with  cocoa  on  linear  or  intercropping plantations;  pure stand  and  sylvopasture).  The  project  is  developed  by  Pur  Development,  the  project  developer  and  owner,  in  association with Aprosacao Association, the core local implementer organization.   Most of the farmers participating in the project are members of Aprosacao, but the project is open to  the  participation  of  other  local  farmers.  Given  the  increasing  number  of members of  the  association  year  by  year,  the  Aprosacao  project  has  been  designed  to  include  additional  modelling  units  in  the  validated  project  area  until  2020,  each  modelling  unit  defined  following  the  year  of  plantation  and  planting model.   ECOCERT  SA  has  conducted  the  first  verification  audit  before  transition  of  the  project  from  the  previous version to the Gold Standard for Global Goals following a request from the project developer  Pur  Development.    The  purpose  of  the  audit  was  to assess  the  extent  to  which  actual  carbon  stocks  reported in the updated project documentation of the Aprosacao project  are materially accurate and  the  extent  to  which  methods  and  procedures,  including  sustainability  monitoring  procedures,  have  been implemented in accordance with the registered project documentation.    To conduct the audit ECOCERT realized a pre-verification assessment consistent in a remote desk audit  of  the  project  documentation  and  supporting  information,  a  field  visit  and  stakeholder  interviews  process and a documentary review. The Project Documentation was assessed against following criteria:  Gold  Standard  for  ISO  14064-2:2006,  Afforestation/Reforestation  (A/R)  Gold  Standard  Requirements  Version 0.9 August 2013, Applicable GS LUF Program Updates, A/R CDM Combined tool to identify the  baseline  scenario  and  demonstrate  additionality  in  A/R  CDM  project  activities  v1.0,  Other  Applicable  A/R CDM tools according to GS requirements, Other applicable guidance according to GS requirements  (e.g. IPCC Guidelines for National GHG Inventories, ILO Conventions).   Following the results of validation  activities described  above 8  CARs,  1  CL  and 3 FAR  were  identified.  The revised final documents submitted to ECOCERT for review resulted in the closure of all open CARs.  The audit team has no restrictions or uncertainties with respect to the compliance of the project with  all the GS A/R standard criteria and requirements. The project is being appropriately implemented and  monitored. CO2 certificates benefit was calculated correctly on the basis of the information contained  in the registered GS5012 project documentation.     In opinion of ECOCERT the actual carbon stocks of the Aprosacao Project for the period 15-June-2012  to  15-December-2018  are  fairly  stated  in  the  updated  CO2-fixation  document  and  ex-post  removals  calculations documents v1.4 dated 30 September 2019.  ECOCERT verified that the CO2 certificates in  the  relevant  reporting  period  are  equivalent  to  14  924  tCO2e  after  buffer  and  confirms  that  this  is  below the maximum project’s expected carbon stocks equivalent to 51 876 validated CO2 certificates  after buffer defined in the GS5012 registered project documentation. | :

In opinion of ECOCERT the actual carbon stocks of the Aprosacao Project for the period 15-June-2012  to  15-December-2018  are  fairly  stated  in  the  updated  CO2-fixation  document  and  ex-post  removals  calculations documents v1.4 dated 30 September 2019.  ECOCERT verified that the CO2 certificates in  the  relevant  reporting  period  are  equivalent  to  14  924  tCO2e  after  buffer  and  confirms  that  this  is  below the maximum project’s expected carbon stocks equivalent to 51 876 validated CO2 certificates  after buffer defined in the GS5012 registered project documentation. | :

What is the total land area where altern.content: updated document do not match the information provided in the baseline and project carbon stocks  calculations documents.: -   o   F   I Client  Response:    The  survey  is  conducted  after  a  minimum  of  3  years  from  the  plantation  completion  which  means  that  more  than  3  years  had  passed  between  plantation  and  leakage  monitoring,  thus  ensuring  that  even  the  leakage  events  that  had  not  happened  right  after  plantations would be taken into account.   -  As a matter of fact, leakage monitoring surveys were mostly conducted in 2018 and 2019 in  the framework of biomass inventories which is consistent with this “more than 3 years” rule.  -  The selection of parcels on which leakage surveys were conducted were selected under the  following criteria:  o  Planting models (pure stand or agroforestry)  o  Previous use of soils (cocoa farming / pasture)  o  Diversity of plantation waves, with parcels from each stratum (the sampling was based on  the selection of parcels used for biomass inventories)  In total 47 farmers were interviewed.    The  main  conclusions  of  leakage  surveys  (around  20%  of  producers  interviewed  -  40%  of  them with Model 3 - 45 % with Model 2 and 15% with Model 4) are the following:    For Model 3, displacement of pre-project activities is not a concern and surveys confirm that  this  practice  is  nil  outside  farmer's  lands  propriety.  In  fact,  a  majority  of  farmers  used  to  adopt  a  rotative fallow/annual crops and availability of land is not an issue in the project area.   o  For Model 2 (Cocoa - perennial crops), as the crops was framed in the context of Aprosacao  cocoa  association  and  market  creation  opportunities  (Halba),  no  displacement  of  pre-project  activities was monitored, as farmers find this new activity more appealing (because more profitable  and more structured by the support of technical team from Aprosacao). They are not displacing but  replacing pre-project activities.  o   For  Model  4,  like  for  Model  3,  interviewed  farmers  have  a  different  land  for  pasture  available, no displacement of pre-project activities was observed.  ollowing this finding and internal discussion on appropriate timing to monitor leakage, the project  proponent  has taken  the decision to systematically integrate  leakage monitoring in the  plantation  follow up procedure in the future (monitoring 2, conducted on each parcel 6 months to 1 year after  plantation). Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth.  UR  Projet  asks  this  CAR  to  become  a  FAR  providing  the  time  needed  to  show  progress  on  this  matter until the next verification.:  | :  | :  | :

Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

updated document do not match the information provided in the baseline and project carbon stocks  calculations documents.: -   o   F   I Client  Response:    The  survey  is  conducted  after  a  minimum  of  3  years  from  the  plantation  completion  which  means  that  more  than  3  years  had  passed  between  plantation  and  leakage  monitoring,  thus  ensuring  that  even  the  leakage  events  that  had  not  happened  right  after  plantations would be taken into account.   -  As a matter of fact, leakage monitoring surveys were mostly conducted in 2018 and 2019 in  the framework of biomass inventories which is consistent with this “more than 3 years” rule.  -  The selection of parcels on which leakage surveys were conducted were selected under the  following criteria:  o  Planting models (pure stand or agroforestry)  o  Previous use of soils (cocoa farming / pasture)  o  Diversity of plantation waves, with parcels from each stratum (the sampling was based on  the selection of parcels used for biomass inventories)  In total 47 farmers were interviewed.    The  main  conclusions  of  leakage  surveys  (around  20%  of  producers  interviewed  -  40%  of  them with Model 3 - 45 % with Model 2 and 15% with Model 4) are the following:    For Model 3, displacement of pre-project activities is not a concern and surveys confirm that  this  practice  is  nil  outside  farmer's  lands  propriety.  In  fact,  a  majority  of  farmers  used  to  adopt  a  rotative fallow/annual crops and availability of land is not an issue in the project area.   o  For Model 2 (Cocoa - perennial crops), as the crops was framed in the context of Aprosacao  cocoa  association  and  market  creation  opportunities  (Halba),  no  displacement  of  pre-project  activities was monitored, as farmers find this new activity more appealing (because more profitable  and more structured by the support of technical team from Aprosacao). They are not displacing but  replacing pre-project activities.  o   For  Model  4,  like  for  Model  3,  interviewed  farmers  have  a  different  land  for  pasture  available, no displacement of pre-project activities was observed.  ollowing this finding and internal discussion on appropriate timing to monitor leakage, the project  proponent  has taken  the decision to systematically integrate  leakage monitoring in the  plantation  follow up procedure in the future (monitoring 2, conducted on each parcel 6 months to 1 year after  plantation). Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

P 2019.  With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth.  UR  Projet  asks  this  CAR  to  become  a  FAR  providing  the  time  needed  to  show  progress  on  this  matter until the next verification.:  | : Farmer | : Land ownership documentation | :

They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth.  UR  Projet  asks  this  CAR  to  become  a  FAR  providing  the  time  needed  to  show  progress  on  this  matter until the next verification.:  | : Oscar Joel Pachecho | : The project owner document indicates in its registry  “certificate under payment” but the document is just  an  invitation  sent  to  the  farmer  in  2017  to  get  his  property document at relevant governmental offices. | :

3. Good health and well being.target: 3.9 By 2030, substantially reduce the number of deaths and illnesses from hazardous chemicals and air, water and soil pollution and contamination
3. Good health and well being.indicator: Area under reduced/avoided open burning of biomass, crop residue

3. Good health and well being.summary: The evidence does not directly measure the indicator variable, 'Number of farmers reduced/avoided open burning of biomass, crop residue'. Although 47 farmers were interviewed and 149 farmers were visited, the context does not provide any information on the adoption of alternative measures to open burning. The leakage monitoring surveys and Project Annual Report Form do not address the specific question of farmers shifting from open burning to alternative measures. Therefore, the evidence is qualitative only, and the score is 1.

updated document do not match the information provided in the baseline and project carbon stocks  calculations documents.: -   o   F   I Client  Response:    The  survey  is  conducted  after  a  minimum  of  3  years  from  the  plantation  completion  which  means  that  more  than  3  years  had  passed  between  plantation  and  leakage  monitoring,  thus  ensuring  that  even  the  leakage  events  that  had  not  happened  right  after  plantations would be taken into account.   -  As a matter of fact, leakage monitoring surveys were mostly conducted in 2018 and 2019 in  the framework of biomass inventories which is consistent with this “more than 3 years” rule.  -  The selection of parcels on which leakage surveys were conducted were selected under the  following criteria:  o  Planting models (pure stand or agroforestry)  o  Previous use of soils (cocoa farming / pasture)  o  Diversity of plantation waves, with parcels from each stratum (the sampling was based on  the selection of parcels used for biomass inventories)  In total 47 farmers were interviewed.    The  main  conclusions  of  leakage  surveys  (around  20%  of  producers  interviewed  -  40%  of  them with Model 3 - 45 % with Model 2 and 15% with Model 4) are the following:    For Model 3, displacement of pre-project activities is not a concern and surveys confirm that  this  practice  is  nil  outside  farmer's  lands  propriety.  In  fact,  a  majority  of  farmers  used  to  adopt  a  rotative fallow/annual crops and availability of land is not an issue in the project area.   o  For Model 2 (Cocoa - perennial crops), as the crops was framed in the context of Aprosacao  cocoa  association  and  market  creation  opportunities  (Halba),  no  displacement  of  pre-project  activities was monitored, as farmers find this new activity more appealing (because more profitable  and more structured by the support of technical team from Aprosacao). They are not displacing but  replacing pre-project activities.  o   For  Model  4,  like  for  Model  3,  interviewed  farmers  have  a  different  land  for  pasture  available, no displacement of pre-project activities was observed.  ollowing this finding and internal discussion on appropriate timing to monitor leakage, the project  proponent  has taken  the decision to systematically integrate  leakage monitoring in the  plantation  follow up procedure in the future (monitoring 2, conducted on each parcel 6 months to 1 year after  plantation). Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

0.8 |  Please list the recent activities,  events and actions related to  the project that summarize the  project’s progress during the  monitoring period this report  refers to.: This may include milestones in  implementation, calibration of equipment,  carrying out of surveys or monitoring  activities, Host Country approvals, etc.  Refer to: Principles and Requirements  5.1.42.(a) | -   -   -   S   D   T   B   K Planting activities:    The  second  monitoring  of  parcels  corresponding  to  the  9th  planting  wave  (2021)  ended  in  November  2022. Second monitoring is focused on the monitoring  of  tree  survival  and  technical  recommendations  for  tree maintenance.    49,998  trees  were  distributed  to  farmers,  corresponding to the 10th planting wave of the Project,  between  June  and  August  2022.  Most  of  the  planted  trees  were  installed  inside  cocoa  parcels  for  shade  benefits.   The activities of Planting Registry for parcels of the  10th  planting  wave  (2022)  started  in  March  and  finished in July 2022. On the other side, the activities  of  the  first  monitoring  for  those  parcels  started  in  November 2022 and finished in March 2023     Key figures:  >  98  participating  producers  (19%  women  and  81%  men)  > 174 planted parcels  > 20,572 alive trees (up to March 2023)       -  The  individual  farm  visits  (pre-registry),  aiming  at  identifying  interested  producers  and  parcels  to  be  planted under the new planting wave (#11, year 2023)  started in March 2023 and are still in process with the  APROSACAO technical team.   The goal was 50.000 trees for planting and 5.000 trees  for replanting to be distributed before August 2023.   upporting documents:  “A.1.1  –  Resumen  actividades  Enero  -  Diciembre  2022_Proyecto Reforestación_APS”  “A.1.2 - POA 2022&2023 APS-PUR”  evelopment of the 2nd wave of long-term monitoring  visits  he  RETO  visit’s  objective  is  to  obtain  accurate  and  updated  information  to  understand  the  current  situation,  objectives  and  constraints  of  each  farmer  and each parcel visited, thus being able to give a more  accurate technical advice.  etween  March  &  December  2022  “Long  term  monitoring - R.E.TO'' visits were made by the technical  team to the farmers of the 3rd, 4th and 5th planting  waves,  using  the  improved  tool  developed  in  2021.  Once  the  visits  were  finished,  the  data  organization  and analysis were carried out using the tool developed  also by PUR Projet Latin America team. The results and  analysis will be obtained in December 2023.  ey figures:

What percentage of farmers who practiced.content: updated document do not match the information provided in the baseline and project carbon stocks  calculations documents.: -   o   F   I Client  Response:    The  survey  is  conducted  after  a  minimum  of  3  years  from  the  plantation  completion  which  means  that  more  than  3  years  had  passed  between  plantation  and  leakage  monitoring,  thus  ensuring  that  even  the  leakage  events  that  had  not  happened  right  after  plantations would be taken into account.   -  As a matter of fact, leakage monitoring surveys were mostly conducted in 2018 and 2019 in  the framework of biomass inventories which is consistent with this “more than 3 years” rule.  -  The selection of parcels on which leakage surveys were conducted were selected under the  following criteria:  o  Planting models (pure stand or agroforestry)  o  Previous use of soils (cocoa farming / pasture)  o  Diversity of plantation waves, with parcels from each stratum (the sampling was based on  the selection of parcels used for biomass inventories)  In total 47 farmers were interviewed.    The  main  conclusions  of  leakage  surveys  (around  20%  of  producers  interviewed  -  40%  of  them with Model 3 - 45 % with Model 2 and 15% with Model 4) are the following:    For Model 3, displacement of pre-project activities is not a concern and surveys confirm that  this  practice  is  nil  outside  farmer's  lands  propriety.  In  fact,  a  majority  of  farmers  used  to  adopt  a  rotative fallow/annual crops and availability of land is not an issue in the project area.   o  For Model 2 (Cocoa - perennial crops), as the crops was framed in the context of Aprosacao  cocoa  association  and  market  creation  opportunities  (Halba),  no  displacement  of  pre-project  activities was monitored, as farmers find this new activity more appealing (because more profitable  and more structured by the support of technical team from Aprosacao). They are not displacing but  replacing pre-project activities.  o   For  Model  4,  like  for  Model  3,  interviewed  farmers  have  a  different  land  for  pasture  available, no displacement of pre-project activities was observed.  ollowing this finding and internal discussion on appropriate timing to monitor leakage, the project  proponent  has taken  the decision to systematically integrate  leakage monitoring in the  plantation  follow up procedure in the future (monitoring 2, conducted on each parcel 6 months to 1 year after  plantation). Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth. CL dated 02/19: T Client  Response:  As  farmers  are  allowed  to  participate  each  year  to  plantation  activities,  the  number  per  year  could  be  different  from  the  total  number of  participants  in the  overall  project  –  239. For example, between plantation year 2012 and plantation year 2013 a same producer could  be  participating  but  he  would  not  be  counted  twice  in  the  final  overall  number  of  unique  participants (239).   his  difference  allows  to  both  have  a  vision  of  the  size  of  each  planting  wave  according  to  the  number  of  participants  per  year  and  of  the  size  of  the  project  according  to  the  number  of  participants in the overall period of the project.

The project proponent's response mentions that leakage monitoring surveys were conducted in 2018 and 2019, but it does not provide information on the time saving associated with cooking time and fuel collection. The context does not provide specific information on the time saving associated with cooking time and fuel collection by households in the target population. Therefore, the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

updated document do not match the information provided in the baseline and project carbon stocks  calculations documents.: -   o   F   I Client  Response:    The  survey  is  conducted  after  a  minimum  of  3  years  from  the  plantation  completion  which  means  that  more  than  3  years  had  passed  between  plantation  and  leakage  monitoring,  thus  ensuring  that  even  the  leakage  events  that  had  not  happened  right  after  plantations would be taken into account.   -  As a matter of fact, leakage monitoring surveys were mostly conducted in 2018 and 2019 in  the framework of biomass inventories which is consistent with this “more than 3 years” rule.  -  The selection of parcels on which leakage surveys were conducted were selected under the  following criteria:  o  Planting models (pure stand or agroforestry)  o  Previous use of soils (cocoa farming / pasture)  o  Diversity of plantation waves, with parcels from each stratum (the sampling was based on  the selection of parcels used for biomass inventories)  In total 47 farmers were interviewed.    The  main  conclusions  of  leakage  surveys  (around  20%  of  producers  interviewed  -  40%  of  them with Model 3 - 45 % with Model 2 and 15% with Model 4) are the following:    For Model 3, displacement of pre-project activities is not a concern and surveys confirm that  this  practice  is  nil  outside  farmer's  lands  propriety.  In  fact,  a  majority  of  farmers  used  to  adopt  a  rotative fallow/annual crops and availability of land is not an issue in the project area.   o  For Model 2 (Cocoa - perennial crops), as the crops was framed in the context of Aprosacao  cocoa  association  and  market  creation  opportunities  (Halba),  no  displacement  of  pre-project  activities was monitored, as farmers find this new activity more appealing (because more profitable  and more structured by the support of technical team from Aprosacao). They are not displacing but  replacing pre-project activities.  o   For  Model  4,  like  for  Model  3,  interviewed  farmers  have  a  different  land  for  pasture  available, no displacement of pre-project activities was observed.  ollowing this finding and internal discussion on appropriate timing to monitor leakage, the project  proponent  has taken  the decision to systematically integrate  leakage monitoring in the  plantation  follow up procedure in the future (monitoring 2, conducted on each parcel 6 months to 1 year after  plantation). Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

They are not displacing but  replacing pre-project activities.  o   For  Model  4,  like  for  Model  3,  interviewed  farmers  have  a  different  land  for  pasture  available, no displacement of pre-project activities was observed.  ollowing this finding and internal discussion on appropriate timing to monitor leakage, the project  proponent  has taken  the decision to systematically integrate  leakage monitoring in the  plantation  follow up procedure in the future (monitoring 2, conducted on each parcel 6 months to 1 year after  plantation). Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. For example, between plantation year 2012 and plantation year 2013 a same producer could  be  participating  but  he  would  not  be  counted  twice  in  the  final  overall  number  of  unique  participants (239).   his  difference  allows  to  both  have  a  vision  of  the  size  of  each  planting  wave  according  to  the  number  of  participants  per  year  and  of  the  size  of  the  project  according  to  the  number  of  participants in the overall period of the project.

The context primarily discusses project activities, stakeholder engagement, and project performance, but does not contain relevant information on employee demographics or roles. Therefore, the evidence fails to satisfy the measurement requirement.

With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth. Clean water and sanitation.summary: The evidence from the sustainability monitoring report to ECOCERT (Document: GS5012+Aprosacao+-+Verification+Report%281%29.pdf, Page: 30) is qualitative only, as it mentions submission of a report but does not provide explicit quantitative data on water retention capacity. Although the project's focus on long-term monitoring and advising programs (Document: GS-5012_PUR_HN_AnnualReport_2023.pdf, Page: 5) suggests some consideration for water management, no direct evidence of the capacity of water systems to retain and manage water resources sustainably is found. Therefore, the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

7. Affordable and clean energy.summary: The evidence for the indicator 'Number of beneficiaries: Households' is weak and indirect. The client response mentions that the number of participants in the overall project is 239, which is likely referring to the total number of unique households (Document: GS5012+Aprosacao+-+Verification+Report.pdf, Page: 31). However, this information is not explicitly stated as a direct measure of the indicator variable. The baseline biomass data for 2015, 2016, 2017, and 2018 can be used to infer the number of households using clean fuels and technologies, but this is also indirect and does not directly measure the indicator variable. Therefore, the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

updated document do not match the information provided in the baseline and project carbon stocks  calculations documents.: -   o   F   I Client  Response:    The  survey  is  conducted  after  a  minimum  of  3  years  from  the  plantation  completion  which  means  that  more  than  3  years  had  passed  between  plantation  and  leakage  monitoring,  thus  ensuring  that  even  the  leakage  events  that  had  not  happened  right  after  plantations would be taken into account.   -  As a matter of fact, leakage monitoring surveys were mostly conducted in 2018 and 2019 in  the framework of biomass inventories which is consistent with this “more than 3 years” rule.  -  The selection of parcels on which leakage surveys were conducted were selected under the  following criteria:  o  Planting models (pure stand or agroforestry)  o  Previous use of soils (cocoa farming / pasture)  o  Diversity of plantation waves, with parcels from each stratum (the sampling was based on  the selection of parcels used for biomass inventories)  In total 47 farmers were interviewed.    The  main  conclusions  of  leakage  surveys  (around  20%  of  producers  interviewed  -  40%  of  them with Model 3 - 45 % with Model 2 and 15% with Model 4) are the following:    For Model 3, displacement of pre-project activities is not a concern and surveys confirm that  this  practice  is  nil  outside  farmer's  lands  propriety.  In  fact,  a  majority  of  farmers  used  to  adopt  a  rotative fallow/annual crops and availability of land is not an issue in the project area.   o  For Model 2 (Cocoa - perennial crops), as the crops was framed in the context of Aprosacao  cocoa  association  and  market  creation  opportunities  (Halba),  no  displacement  of  pre-project  activities was monitored, as farmers find this new activity more appealing (because more profitable  and more structured by the support of technical team from Aprosacao). They are not displacing but  replacing pre-project activities.  o   For  Model  4,  like  for  Model  3,  interviewed  farmers  have  a  different  land  for  pasture  available, no displacement of pre-project activities was observed.  ollowing this finding and internal discussion on appropriate timing to monitor leakage, the project  proponent  has taken  the decision to systematically integrate  leakage monitoring in the  plantation  follow up procedure in the future (monitoring 2, conducted on each parcel 6 months to 1 year after  plantation). Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

Further leakage monitoring will also be included in long-term follow up visits planned in  the Long-term contract (see answer to CAR 1 and 2).   n particular regarding fuel use and timber collection by farmers, the project is generating additional  sources  of  fuel  wood  and timber through  thinning,  pruning  and  harvesting  of  trees  in  accordance  with  a  Sustainable  Forestry  Management  Plan  preserving  project’s  carbon  stocks.  2019  project

7. Affordable and clean energy.summary: The retrieved context does not provide any information related to access to clean energy options, which is the focus of the SDG indicator. The context primarily discusses land ownership, training, and gender equity, as well as project implementation and verification of greenhouse gas emissions reductions. No supporting evidence was found to assess the extent to which individuals have access to clean energy options. Therefore, the evidence fails to satisfy the measurement requirement of the indicator.

8. Decent work and economic growth.summary: The evidence does not directly measure the total number of jobs generated as a result of the project. Although the context mentions that the project intends to generate about 18,655 jobs and 14,924 jobs in different years, these values are not explicitly stated as the total number of jobs generated. The context also mentions additional 263 ha have been planted in 2016 and 2018, but this information does not directly relate to the total number of jobs generated. Therefore, the evidence is weak and indirect, and the score is 0.

What is the geographic scope of the jobs.content: P 2019.  With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth.  UR  Projet  asks  this  CAR  to  become  a  FAR  providing  the  time  needed  to  show  progress  on  this  matter until the next verification.:  | : Farmer | : Land ownership documentation | :

More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth. 8. Decent work and economic growth.target: 8.5 By 2030, achieve full and productive employment...

8. Decent work and economic growth.summary: No supporting evidence was found in the provided context to determine the total number of employees earning above the local minimum wage in the project. The context repeatedly mentions the number of farmers participating in the project, which is stated to be 239, but does not provide information on employee wages or the local minimum wage. The clarification requests and responses in the verification reports focus on the difference between the number of participants per year and the total number of participants in the overall project, but do not contain relevant information on employee wages or labor laws. Therefore, the evidence does not directly measure the indicator variable, and the score is 0.

With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth.  UR  Projet  asks  this  CAR  to  become  a  FAR  providing  the  time  needed  to  show  progress  on  this  matter until the next verification.:  | : Rina Ponce | : The  document  is  under  a  different  name:  Jose  Ruperto Euceda Perdomo, different from the project  participant | :

Responsible consumption and producti.summary: No supporting evidence was found in the provided context to calculate the average reduction in synthetic fertilizer consumption per ha in the target region. The context provides information on the rate of application of fertilizers (100 kgN/ha/application) and the duration of use of fertilizers (3 years), but does not provide any data on fertilizer consumption or reduction. The context also mentions total cumulated emissions of nitrogen fertilizer for specific years (2012-2017), but this information is not relevant to calculating the average reduction in synthetic fertilizer consumption. Therefore, the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

Responsible consumption and producti.summary: The evidence from the Do-no-harm assessment (Document: GS5012+Aprosacao+-+Verification+Report%281%29.pdf, Page: 22) mentions measures for collection of waste products and avoiding erosion by compaction of soils, which is related to environmentally sound management practices. However, this evidence does not directly measure the indicator variable 'Average reduction (%) in pesticide used per ha'. The project owner's clarification that parameters linked to SDG identified by the project owner match main parameters defined to monitor potential impacts identified in the LSC at initial certification (Document: GS5012+Aprosacao+-+Verification+Report.pdf, Page: 30) also does not provide quantitative data on pesticide reduction. Therefore, the evidence is qualitative and does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement.

Climate action.summary: The evidence from the Verification Report (GS5012+Aprosacao+-+Verification+Report%281%29.pdf, Page: 21) and Verification Report (Verification+Report_monitoring+period_15.06.2012+to+15.12.2018_v2.pdf, Page: 21) suggests that climate change measures are integrated into national policies and practices. However, the evidence does not explicitly mention the amount of GHGs emissions avoided or sequestered. The mention of 'management procedures to ensure consistency and accuracy in determining GHG emission reductions and removals' and 'adequate monitoring system with specific procedures for collecting, aggregating, and recording data' is qualitative and does not directly measure the indicator variable. Therefore, the evidence satisfies the indicator's measurement requirement partially, but the score is 1 due to the lack of explicit quantitative data.

15. Life on land.summary: The evidence does not provide explicit quantitative data to directly measure the total area of forest land under sustainable management practices. The context mentions the planting of additional 263 ha in 2016 and 2018, but it does not provide information on the rate of increase in the area of forest land under sustainable management practices over time. The types of forest management practices being implemented are largely focused on Sustainable Timber management, and the key factors influencing the adoption of sustainable forest management practices among forest landowners/operators appear to be land tenure, profitability, and market creation opportunities. However, the evidence does not directly measure the indicator variable, and the numbers mentioned do not satisfy the measurement requirement.

P 2019.  With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020. With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth. 15. Life on land.indicator: Forest areas managed sustainably for forest products including sustainable produced fuelwood

15. Life on land.summary: The provided evidence does not directly measure the total above and below ground biomass stock in forests within a specific geographic scope. Although the total above ground biomass stock is calculated as 136.5 tC02/ha (GS5012+Aprosacao+-+Verification+Report%281%29.pdf, Page: 16), the below ground biomass stock cannot be directly calculated due to the lack of explicit values. The evidence for below ground biomass is limited to specific strata, such as cocoa and other (SB4) and pastures and degraded fallows (SB3 and SB2), but does not provide a comprehensive total. Therefore, the evidence fails to satisfy the measurement requirement of the indicator.

15. Life on land.summary: The evidence does not directly measure the indicator variable 'Total area of Trees Planted'. Although the context mentions that the project has expanded its scope over time, the exact land area covered by the project is not specified. The mention of 'additional modelling units' and 'planting model' is qualitative and does not provide explicit quantitative data. Therefore, the evidence fails to satisfy the measurement requirement.

What is the extent of forest restoration.content: :  | : The Aprosacao Reforestation Project is a community project implemented in small scale farms located  in  the  region  of  Olancho,  Honduras.  The  project  aims  to  bring  multiple  social  and  environmental  benefits  by  restoring  forest  cover  in  pastures  and  degraded  areas,  under  four  different  models  of  plantation  (agroforestry  combined  with  cocoa  on  linear  or  intercropping plantations;  pure stand  and  sylvopasture).  The  project  is  developed  by  Pur  Development,  the  project  developer  and  owner,  in  association with Aprosacao Association, the core local implementer organization.   Most of the farmers participating in the project are members of Aprosacao, but the project is open to  the  participation  of  other  local  farmers.  Given  the  increasing  number  of members of  the  association  year  by  year,  the  Aprosacao  project  has  been  designed  to  include  additional  modelling  units  in  the  validated  project  area  until  2020,  each  modelling  unit  defined  following  the  year  of  plantation  and  planting model.   ECOCERT  SA  has  conducted  the  first  verification  audit  before  transition  of  the  project  from  the  previous version to the Gold Standard for Global Goals following a request from the project developer  Pur  Development.    The  purpose  of  the  audit  was  to assess  the  extent  to  which  actual  carbon  stocks  reported in the updated project documentation of the Aprosacao project  are materially accurate and  the  extent  to  which  methods  and  procedures,  including  sustainability  monitoring  procedures,  have  been implemented in accordance with the registered project documentation.    To conduct the audit ECOCERT realized a pre-verification assessment consistent in a remote desk audit  of  the  project  documentation  and  supporting  information,  a  field  visit  and  stakeholder  interviews  process and a documentary review. The Project Documentation was assessed against following criteria:  Gold  Standard  for  ISO  14064-2:2006,  Afforestation/Reforestation  (A/R)  Gold  Standard  Requirements  Version 0.9 August 2013, Applicable GS LUF Program Updates, A/R CDM Combined tool to identify the  baseline  scenario  and  demonstrate  additionality  in  A/R  CDM  project  activities  v1.0,  Other  Applicable  A/R CDM tools according to GS requirements, Other applicable guidance according to GS requirements  (e.g. IPCC Guidelines for National GHG Inventories, ILO Conventions).   Following the results of validation  activities described  above 8  CARs,  1  CL  and 3 FAR  were  identified.  The revised final documents submitted to ECOCERT for review resulted in the closure of all open CARs.  The audit team has no restrictions or uncertainties with respect to the compliance of the project with  all the GS A/R standard criteria and requirements. The project is being appropriately implemented and  monitored. CO2 certificates benefit was calculated correctly on the basis of the information contained  in the registered GS5012 project documentation.     In opinion of ECOCERT the actual carbon stocks of the Aprosacao Project for the period 15-June-2012  to  15-December-2018  are  fairly  stated  in  the  updated  CO2-fixation  document  and  ex-post  removals  calculations documents v1.4 dated 30 September 2019.  ECOCERT verified that the CO2 certificates in  the  relevant  reporting  period  are  equivalent  to  14  924  tCO2e  after  buffer  and  confirms  that  this  is  below the maximum project’s expected carbon stocks equivalent to 51 876 validated CO2 certificates  after buffer defined in the GS5012 registered project documentation. | :

P 2019.  With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth.  UR  Projet  asks  this  CAR  to  become  a  FAR  providing  the  time  needed  to  show  progress  on  this  matter until the next verification.:  | : Oscar Joel Pachecho | : The project owner document indicates in its registry  “certificate under payment” but the document is just  an  invitation  sent  to  the  farmer  in  2017  to  get  his  property document at relevant governmental offices. | :

15. Life on land.summary: The evidence does not directly measure the total protected land area. Although the project area is mentioned to be 1,000 ha of additional forest cover (Document: GS5012+Aprosacao+-+Verification+Report.pdf, Page: 14), the total land area designated as protected is not explicitly stated. The percentage of the project area that is protected land area cannot be determined with the given information. The data unit used to measure the protected land area is hectares (ha), but the evidence does not satisfy the indicator's measurement requirement. The minimum percentage of project area that must be protected to meet the indicator threshold is not explicitly stated, but a materiality threshold of 5% is mentioned for small scale GS project activities (Document: GS5012+Aprosacao+-+Verification+Report.pdf, Page: 6).

C AR1 dated 02/19:: A   T   B Client  Response:    During  the  audit  review  it  appeared  that  for  some  producers  no  evidence  of  property of land could be found available at the Aprosacao office.   fter the audit, local project teams have worked on the topic and the team is now able to provide  physical evidence of land property in Aprosacao office for 65% of the producers from the sampling  selected during the audit (see supporting documents 1. attached).  he sampling is hence representative of the current status of the project progress on land tenure,  that is: to date, in the Aprosacao office, evidence of property of land for the project participants is  available for more than 70% of them (see supporting documents 4. attached).  esides, to better meet the standard requirements on land property, a complementary strategy has  been elaborated and will continue  to be  implemented to generate  improvements  before  the  next  certification.   -  Firstly,  the  project  now  has a document giving a clear view of land tenure status at office  level.   -  In addition, for the next period of activities framed in the first Long Term Activities Contract  between  PUR  Projet  and  Aprosacao  (May  2019  –  August  2020)  a  special  referent  was  designated  inside  the  Reforestation  Project  to  follow  up  on  the  subject.  More  than  377  parcels will be visited, and more than 24 workshops will be organized, one of the objectives  of such visits being to obtain a copy of evidence of property when lacking.   -  The  appointed  Coordinator  is  also  in  charge  of  following  up  on  the  topic  in  direct  coordination  with  the  local  governmental  entity  in  charge  of  legal  property  of  land  in  HONDURAS,  the  INA. More  regular meetings  have  been  set  up  since  May 2019  to  allow  a  constant  follow  up  and  push  for  a  prioritized  land  titling  in  the  project’s  area  and  an  increased  support  of  Aprosacao  by  the  INA.  Efforts  already  made  in  2018  proved  the  efficiency of such strategy, as a total of 17 land title files were prepared and published by  the INA from a total of 63  Aprosacao  farmers visited. The plan  in 2019 is to increase  land  tenure obtention through an even closer coordination with the INA.   -  On top of that PUR Projet successfully obtained the support of the European Union through  the  international  cooperation  program  EUROCLIMA+  that  started  at  the  end  of  January

Verification Report 
GS5012-Aprosacao Project 
 
 
 
 
2 
 
Summary: 
The Aprosacao Reforestation Project is a community project implemented in small scale farms located 
in the region of Olancho, Honduras.
The project aims to bring multiple social and environmental 
benefits by restoring forest cover in pastures and degraded areas, under four different models of

15. Life on land.summary: No supporting evidence was found to determine the extent to which terrestrial ecosystems, specifically forests, maintain their inherent biodiversity. The context mentions various documents and references related to biomass inventories, allometric equations, and wood densities of tropical tree species, but none of these provide information on tree species diversity. The documents provided focus on leakage surveys, diversity of plantation waves, and biomass inventory plots, but do not contain relevant data on tree species diversity. The evidence does not directly measure the indicator variable, and therefore, the score is 0.

For example, between plantation year 2012 and plantation year 2013 a same producer could 
be participating but he would not be counted twice in the final overall number of unique 
participants (239).

Prior to verification statement 
Client Response: As farmers are allowed to participate each year to plantation activities, the 
number per year could be different from the total number of participants in the overall project – 
239.
For example, between plantation year 2012 and plantation year 2013 a same producer could 
be participating but he would not be counted twice in the final overall number of unique 
participants (239).

With  a  global  objective  defined  as  "Improving  the  governance  and  management  of  land  use  to  address  the  causes  of  forest  loss  and  degradation  and  the  improvement  of  carbon stocks  in Honduras and Peru", land tenure is a key  focus of the  Euroclima+ Project  097.  One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth.  UR  Projet  asks  this  CAR  to  become  a  FAR  providing  the  time  needed  to  show  progress  on  this  matter until the next verification.:  | : José Porfidio Hernandez Moradel | : Land  ownership  in  the  record  is  under  a  different  name, not the project participant | :

One  of  PUR  Projet’s  local  partner  for  EUROCLIMA+,  Fundation  Helvetas  Honduras,  includes a management team of agronomists and technicians who are engaged in activities  of  assistance,  development  and  monitoring  of  project  actions,  in  cooperation  with  participating farmers and farmer groups. They are in charge of implementing these activities  in  the  project  area  with  Aprosacao  farmers  before  July  2020.  More  precisely,  the  EUROCLIMA+ plan of activities includes:   1- Design of a plan for clarification and regularization of land tenure in the 3 territories of the action  that includes research, training and dissemination of information on the right to land ownership of  women and youth. – special team and special budget will be dedicated   2-  At  least  one  training  workshop  on  gender  and  social  equity  on  the  right  to  land  ownership  of  women and youth.  UR  Projet  asks  this  CAR  to  become  a  FAR  providing  the  time  needed  to  show  progress  on  this  matter until the next verification.:  | : Carlos Alberto Hernandez | : Property documentation on the record | :
